paragraphs.body: We live in a world where women are asked to choose between a bear and a man to be alone in a forest, and the overbearing majority choose the bear. According to The Quint, the debate all started from a TikTok account named Screenshot HQ. The video asked women, “Would you rather be stuck in a forest with a man or a bear?” seven out of eight women in the video chose the bear. I mean, how ridiculous is that? I honestly laugh at the fact that bears have more trust from women than a man will ever receive. This trend has gone viral ever since, and it has even become a meme. Whenever a man does something questionable or misogynistic, the first comment you see from a woman is, “Yup, I choose the bear.” The real kicker is the fact that misogyny isn’t even a new or current topic. The battle between men and women has been around since the moment God decided to create Eve from the ribs of Adam. Violence against women has surged throughout history, and there doesn’t seem to be an end to it any time soon. It has come to the point where a situation with a zoo animal would be more reasonable than the horror of a man’s desires.

annotations.comment: Yes! Great job with the context :)

paragraphs.body: My role as a woman is to bear. Bear, a verb in the Old English beran, means “to carry, bring; bring forth, give birth to, produce; to endure, to wear.” According to etymonline.com, My role as a woman Is to carry my husband’s child, produce an heir, and bring forth the next generation. Women bear children for nine months to then be laid on a hospital bed for 12-24 hours, waiting in pain so venomous that it is expected that one belt out for her punishment to end. Women bear the fact that if they are not a virgin, they are sluts. Women must bear traits of humility, kindness, and selflessness. Women bear the anxiety of walking down a dark street with their keys between their knuckles, singing a lullaby to calm their nerves as they feel a figure follow behind them. Women bear the responsibilities of cooking and cleaning while also maintaining a respectable profession. Women bear a man’s need for them to look presentable but have no make-up on and look like supermodels, but you must eat well or else nobody will want to court you. Women bear being hot and cute, sexy and sweet, blonder and more brunette, strong and submissive. Women bear wanting nothing but giving everything. On top of all of this, women bear the weight of simply being women, nothing more, nothing less.

annotations.comment: Fab paragraph!! The way you define "bear" in so many different ways is awesome.

paragraphs.body: On a famous TV program called The Graham Norton Show, Saoirse Ronan, a notable actress, brought light to the violence women face. It started with Paul Mescal, a man credited with having been written by a woman, making an innocent joke about how it wouldn’t be normal to be thinking about ways to protect yourself on the street from others. He and the other men on the panel laughed at how silly it must be to think, “Oh! Let me grab my phone to protect myself.” As they continued to cackle at such a comical idea, Saoirse Ronan politely waited until she could get a word in. She reminded the men and the audience that women have to think about it almost every day. This proves to me how deeply rooted sexism is. Not to mention the fact that the room went silent after Saoirse’s comment. Even the most trusted and harmless men don’t understand the weight women bear in their everyday lives, and it has become such an awkward topic. It has become second nature for women to feel cautious when they are walking alone. That should not be normal.

annotations.text: Even the most trusted and harmless men don’t understand the weight women bear in their everyday lives, and it has become such an awkward topic. It has become second nature for women to feel cautious when they are walking alone. That should not be normal.

paragraphs.body: Bear, as a noun in the Old English bera, is a “large carnivorous or omnivorous mammal of the family Ursidae.” According to etymonline.com. A bear, the embodiment of the wild, operates primarily on instinct. It does not play games or engage in manipulative behaviors. It acts upon its need for survival. A bear’s actions are straightforward and transparent. It may be dangerous if threatened, but it will not deceive, manipulate, or feed on the emotional vulnerabilities of others. It will leave you alone or respond based on its instincts, which are predictable and rooted in survival. A bear will kill you; a man will touch your leg inappropriately during music class in the seventh grade, grazing his fingers along your thigh without your consent. A bear will kill you; a man will corner you and force you to make out in the quiet gym with no cameras around to trace evidence. A bear will kill you; a man will send you a dick pic and make you compliment him on how big he is. A bear will kill you; a man will take away your rights to your body.

paragraphs.body: After the most recent presidential election, I have come to quarrel with boys who felt the need to argue with my views. They went to the extent of calling one of the candidates an Antichrist and using the restriction of the bodily autonomy of women as their argument to prove their point. As a response to his blasphemy, I had to remind these boys of their privilege. I told him that a man who has no idea what It feels like to be a woman has no right to talk about what women get to do with their bodies. As a woman, I’ve grown tired of being expected to bear these conversations, to carry the emotional weight of educating those who cannot or will not listen. We are not a policy that can be voted upon nor government property. That night of the election results, I scrolled through my social media and witnessed a man talk about how men have won again, how it was now “your body, my choice.” I found myself on my bathroom floor that night, tears swelling up in my eyes as I silently pleaded with the Lord above to save me from my country. With no one to be comforted by, I sat alone. Tears fell on my cheeks, snot clogged my nose, unable to breathe. I made a promise to the universe that night that I would not be born a woman in my next life.

annotations.text: As a woman, I’ve grown tired of being expected to bear these conversations, to carry the emotional weight of educating those who cannot or will not listen. We are not a policy that can be voted upon nor government property.

paragraphs.body: Choosing a bear rather than a man in a forest serves as a pitiful metaphor, serving as a reflection of the burdens women carry daily, but through it all, it is oddly fitting that women would choose a bear over a man. It’s as if women choose the bear because the bear would lift everything that women are forced to bear. If a woman chose the man, she would have to bear the questions of who it is, how deep in the forest they are, what they have on them, and if it is during the day or night. The bear is merely a bear. Not a friend nor a foe. They are just bears. The choice is yours. Would you rather be stuck in a forest with a man or a bear? Work Cited bear | Search Online Etymology Dictionary.
